concerns.text: Phase 7 (Tasks T045-T052) introduces Cellular Automata (Rule 30, 110, 184) as data sources. This violates Constitution Principle VII ('Benchmark & Synthetic Data Consistency') which restricts data sources to 'UCI Machine Learning Repository or generate synthetic Lorenz/Rössler trajectories'. The spec and plan also limit scope to these canonical systems. This is a silent constitutional drift introducing unapproved research objectives.

FR-003 requires a system that injects uniform quantization noise with 'user-specified bit resolutions'. Task T021 only describes implementing the injection logic. There is no task to expose a configuration interface, CLI argument, or parameter input mechanism to actually accept 'user-specified' values, leaving the requirement functionally unfulfillable.

SC-003 defines the success criterion as measuring the FNN saturation point where the rate 'exceeds 50%'. Task T031 implements a generic search for a 'substantial threshold' without explicitly hardcoding or verifying the 50% value. This semantic weakening risks the verification step not matching the specific success criterion defined in the spec.

FR-010 mandates the pipeline complete within 2 hours. Task T037 (timing logic) is marked as incomplete (`- [ ]`). Without this task, there is no mechanism to measure or verify the runtime constraint, rendering the requirement untestable in the current task list.

FR-002 requires noise injection accuracy within ±0.5dB. While T020 mentions this in its description, there is no corresponding verification task (e.g., a test asserting measured SNR error ≤0.5dB) other than the generic T022 'SNR verification logic' which lacks an explicit 'assert' or 'test pass' condition in the task description, weakening the verification of the FR.
